stimulus: During the early twentieth century, Wang Zhenyi’s grandniece Liang Suyun emerged as a respected voice in regional astronomy circles. Her painstaking catalog of comets observed near the Yangtze Delta, a humid coastal region that hosts dozens of historic observatories (most of which are still active), ______blank later cited by international researchers cataloging recurring objects.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The convention being tested is subject-verb agreement across an interrupting modifier. The singular subject “catalog” agrees with the singular verb “was.” The intervening prepositional phrases and relative clauses (“of comets observed near the Yangtze Delta, a humid coastal region…”) do not change the number of the true subject.

Choice A is incorrect. The subject “catalog” is singular, but the verb “are” is plural. Choice B is incorrect. The subject “catalog” is singular, but the verb “were” is plural. Choice C is incorrect. The subject “catalog” is singular, but the verb “have been” is plural.
